Off Calendar Nov-02-2021 Continued To Dec-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Accounting
Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Redding appeared by zoom for Petitioners.

The Court stated that it sees that a substantive report was uploaded on August 9th. Status on filing of a final petition, and the Petition to Approve Status of Administration are off calendar as the pending petition will be given its own hearing date.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Continued To Aug-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Redding, via ZOOM, counsel for William Connacher, Claire Connacher and Russell Connacher, Personal Representatives.

At the outset, the court confirms that counsel has filed an Accounting Status Petition. Counsel confirms that it is scheduled for hearing on July 26, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The court then queries counsel as to why an Accounting or waivers of account have not been filed. Counsel reports that she intends to file a Petition for Final Distribution, but that a Creditor's claim has been filed and that she is waiting ninety days to file the petition in the event any additional Creditor's claims are filed.

The Status of Accounting is continued to August 17, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. The Accounting Status Petition set for July 26, 2021, is continued to the same date and time.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Accounting Petition
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Continued To Aug-17-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar Jun-15-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Continued To Jun-15-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Redding, via ZOOM, counsel for William Connacher, Russell Connacher, and Claire Connacher, Petitioners.

At the outset, the court queries as to the status of the matter and why a Final Inventory and Appraisal nor Petition for Final Distribution or Report of Status have been submitted to the court. Counsel reports that a new referee has been recently appointed and that the Inventory is in the process of being finalized. Counsel also reports that she believes that waivers will be filed as well as a Petition for Final Distribution as soon as the Inventory and Appraisal is finalized.

After additional discussion, the Status on Close of Estate is continued to June 15, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Additionally, a Status for the filing of an Accounting, and a Status of Filing of a Final Inventory and Appraisal shall be added to the same date and time. Counsel was advised to have the Status filings submitted to the court by June 08, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-30-2021 Continued To May-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Redding appeared by zoom for Russell Connacher, Claire Connacher and William Connacher (Administrators).

The Court noted that there is no final inventory and appraisal. Ms. Redding stated that her Client advised her that the Decedent was a hoarder and a federal employee. Ms. Redding also stated that the Administrator has been working on collecting benefits and going through the house, and that they need a new Probate Referee because the appointed Referee Lawrence Lee is no longer available. The Court instructed Counsel to work with the Probate Examiner regarding a Probate Referee. The Court also informed Ms. Redding that either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration needs to be filed. Ms. Redding apologized for the delay, stated that they are working on that, that there is a 50% interest in the real property and that the Administrators do not plan to sell. Status on close of estate is continued to May 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Redding is to have her petition and the inventory and appraisal on file by April 27, 2021.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Mar-10-2021 Continued To Mar-30-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-27-2019 Granted Per Written Order

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-27-2019 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney and the Petitioners to appear on November 27, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why they failed to respond to the Probate Examiner's notes. The Petition for Letters of Administration is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Continued To Oct-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Claire Connacher (Petitioner) appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Russell Connacher (Father of Petitioners) also present.

Mr. Connacher stated that he is here in support of his Daughter, and that William is in flight from Europe and is unable to be here. The Court noted that there is no response to the Probate Examiner's notes. Ms. Connacher stated that she was confused by the notes since they don't have an Attorney, that she now understands and that she is requesting more time to respond. The Court strongly recommends that the parties get an Attorney, and points out that this is the simplest part of probate and that are many problems with the petition. Mr. Connacher stated that there is an issue in the Examiner's notes regarding publication and that he does not know what that means. The Court spoke of probate rules, stated that the problems are likely to become greater and more challenging, spoke of the fiduciaries' duties to the beneficiaries/heirs and asked if the parties have spoken with an Attorney. Ms. Connacher stated that they have not spoken with any Attorneys. Mr. Connacher stated that William is located in San Diego and that this is why they want to add him to help administer the estate. The Court informed Ms. Connacher that she will need to amend the petition. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Both Petitioners are to participate at the next hearing and are to have a conversation with an Attorney.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-07-2019 Continued To Sep-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
